reply_content:
So let me get this straight. When DC drops Batman Beyond, it's a masterpiece. When Marvel goes full multiverse with Spider-Verse, it's a standing ovation. But the moment Devil May Cry 5 dares to play with its own formula or give Dante a new coat of paint, the internet starts hyperventilating into a brown paper bag?
Look, it’s okay to evolve a franchise. Characters like Dante aren’t museum exhibits – they’re stories in motion. It’s not sacrilege to explore a different tone or timeline. That’s literally what kept comic books from going extinct. If fans can accept 47 versions of Spider-Man (including a pig), maybe they can chill and let Devil May Cry try something new without assuming the world is ending.
Variety isn't betrayal, folks. It’s spice. And if Dante shows up with a different haircut or a twist in the lore, maybe—just maybe—it’s not the end of the world. Maybe it’s just fun.

reply_content:
I can tell you the basics because if we want to talk about everything Dante can do according to the lore, it would be like a bible.
I will talk first about the world of DMC
There is the human world and the Demonic world, the human one is simple and small, compared to the underworld.
The structure of the underworld is of almost incalculable dimensions and it is an infinite underworld.
That said, can I start with: His strength is capable of rivaling beings capable of raising and destroying the entire underworld, one of them is Nighmare, a creature created by Mundus, who when creating Nighmare had to seal part of his power so as not to end up revealing himself.
In Speed, well, in animation it is fast, but in itself it is faster than the speed of light, even surpassing omnipresence.
Since there are demons that travel through the underworld in one day, and Dante surpasses them all, also his brother Vergil of course.
He has an instant regenerative capacity, and because of his soul (his soul is 9-dimensional, which can revive him from absolute disintegration if he has a motivation to continue living)
It can transcend space-time, reality and even concepts beyond the imagination
since he can draw their names from the demons so that they can never revive
basically it transcends almost everything
can manipulate time and the metaphysical
That's the basics of Dante, I'm not the one who knows the most about the DMC scale, so I may not know much but that's what I know.🤷
and his brother Vergil is almost the same, only he has a Katana called Yamato, which is a Katana capable of cutting space-time, reality, concepts and more and can also make portals to the underworld with a single fragment of it
